snippets.title: Leakage-safe filter + classifier in one Pipeline
snippets.code:
from sklearn.datasets import load_breast_cancer
from sklearn.feature_selection import SelectKBest, mutual_info_classif
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline

X, y = load_breast_cancer(return_X_y=True)

pipe = Pipeline([
    ("select", SelectKBest(score_func=mutual_info_classif, k=15)),
    ("clf", LogisticRegression(max_iter=5000)),
])

scores = cross_val_score(pipe, X, y, cv=5, scoring="roc_auc")
print("cv roc_auc mean:", scores.mean())
snippets.explanation: The selector is refit inside each CV fold on training rows only. This is the baseline pattern for production-safe filter selection before model training.

snippets.title: RFECV to pick subset size without peeking at test
snippets.code:
from sklearn.datasets import load_breast_cancer
from sklearn.feature_selection import RFECV
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

X, y = load_breast_cancer(return_X_y=True)

base = Pipeline([
    ("scale", StandardScaler()),
    ("clf", LogisticRegression(max_iter=5000)),
])

cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=0)
rfe = RFECV(estimator=base, step=1, cv=cv, scoring="roc_auc", n_jobs=-1)
rfe.fit(X, y)

print("n_features_selected:", rfe.n_features_)
print("support sum:", rfe.support_.sum())
snippets.explanation: `RFECV` wraps cross-validation around RFE to choose how many features to keep using only training data passed to `fit`. Scale before coefficient-based elimination when using linear models.

snippets.title: Embedded selection with SelectFromModel (Random Forest)
snippets.code:
from sklearn.datasets import load_breast_cancer
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.feature_selection import SelectFromModel
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score

X, y = load_breast_cancer(return_X_y=True)
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    X, y, test_size=0.2, random_state=0, stratify=y
)

pipe = Pipeline([
    (
        "select",
        SelectFromModel(
            RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=100, random_state=0),
            threshold="median",
        ),
    ),
    ("clf", RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=100, random_state=0)),
])

pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)
print("selected:", pipe.named_steps["select"].get_support().sum())
print("test acc:", accuracy_score(y_test, pipe.predict(X_test)))
snippets.explanation: Forest importances drive embedded selection; the same pipeline object can be serialized for inference. Treat importances as exploratory—validate held-out performance, not importance ranks alone.
